meta.nextUpdate: auto — every 2 hours via GitHub Actions

countries.name: France
countries.note: 1 confirmed — 5 isolated at Bichat hospital, 22 contacts monitored. Quarantine decree: 15-42 days.

countries.name: Netherlands
countries.note: Dutch couple among first deaths. Cases 5 & 6 evacuated to Amsterdam. Ship registry.

countries.name: Germany
countries.note: Case 4 — German national died on board May 2. 4 passengers repatriated to Frankfurt.

countries.name: Switzerland
countries.note: Case 7 — disembarked St Helena Apr 22, hospitalized in Zurich May 1.

countries.name: United Kingdom
countries.note: Case 8 — disembarked Tristan da Cunha Apr 14, probable case under monitoring.

countries.name: South Africa
countries.note: Case 3 — ICU in Johannesburg. PCR confirmed by NICD.

countries.name: Argentina
countries.note: Origin of outbreak. Endemic Andes virus. Patient zero exposed here before boarding.

countries.name: United States
countries.note: Endemic Sin Nombre virus (Four Corners). 1 American passenger tested positive.

countries.name: Spain
countries.note: Port of Tenerife — disembarkation hub. Coordinating medical evacuations.

countries.name: Cape Verde
countries.note: Ship moored off coast. Samples sent to Institut Pasteur de Dakar.

countries.name: Canada
countries.note: Contact tracing ongoing for returning passengers.

timeline.detail: French female passenger tests positive. 5 French nationals isolated at Hopital Bichat (Paris). 22 contacts identified. Quarantine decree: 15-42 days. MV Hondius departs Tenerife to Rotterdam.

timeline.event: MV Hondius arrives at Tenerife — international disembarkation
timeline.detail: Port of Granadilla (05:30). Passengers from 23 nationalities repatriated. 4 Germans flown to Frankfurt. 1 American tested positive. Evacuations coordinated by WHO + ECDC.
6 confirmed + 2 probable cases. All lab-confirmed cases identified as Andes virus by PCR/sequencing. Risk: Low globally, Moderate on ship.

timeline.event: UK Army parachutes onto Tristan da Cunha
timeline.detail: British Army drops medical team to remote island to assist Case 8 (probable).

timeline.event: Andes virus confirmed in all 6 lab cases
timeline.detail: Genomic sequencing at NICD South Africa and Geneva University Hospitals. Argentina publishes patient zero travel history.
First official WHO outbreak notice. Risk assessed as low globally.

timeline.event: Case 4 dies on board — WHO notified
timeline.detail: German national (female). Symptoms onset Apr 28. PCR confirmed at NICD South Africa.
Adult male, symptoms Apr 24. PCR+ confirmed May 2. Andes virus confirmed by sequencing.
Wife of patient zero. Disembarked St Helena Apr 24. Died at emergency department. PCR confirmed May 4.

timeline.event: Case 1 dies on board — Patient zero
timeline.detail: Dutch male. Symptoms from Apr 6 (fever, headache, diarrhoea, respiratory distress). Exposed to rodents during birdwatching in Argentina.
147 people on board (88 passengers, 59 crew), 23 nationalities. Itinerary: Antarctica, South Georgia, Tristan da Cunha, St Helena, Ascension Island, Cape Verde.
